Key Takeaways:
o The Pre-Prelaunch or as we’d like say, call it the pre-pre and as the name
implies, happens before your Prelaunch even starts.
o This is really under the radar before anyone even knows what’s going on.
o This is the pre-pre, this is where you really identify objections and
gather testimonials.
o Your going to want to use the Pre-Prelaunch to warm your list.
o You might even start attracting partners and really, it’s about
starting to build buzz ahead of the launch. This is where you lay the
groundwork for your launch.
o We usually start off with what I call “the shot across the bow” - old naval term
for when they would fire a shot in front of the other boat to warn them that
something is coming.
o The essence is to let them know something is coming but not try to sell them. The
message is that the product is not quite ready yet.
o A big thing to keep in mind is that you want to avoid the “red zone” of people’s BS
detectors. When there’s no hint of a pitch and you're excited about releasing
something you get good response - it’s much softer.
o The characteristics of buzz
o In my market (Internet Marketing), everyone is highly connected:
▪ Lots of forums
▪ Lots of social media
▪ People are on all the email lists
▪ All this needs to be taken into account when I do my Pre-Prelaunch

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o People are not interested in sharing your sale or advertising with people
o It’s important to be authentic and create value - the more you do this the
quicker the buzz will spread
o I like to hit multiple modalities - talk about it on stage, on social media,
different places
o Think of this in terms of Concentric Circles:
▪ PLF and my team in the middle
▪ First I notify the closest people to me (PLF Owners) - usually the
first hint comes to this group
▪ Then Joint Venture Partners
▪ Then social media following - inherently very shareable
▪ Then my prospect list
▪ Then people on JV’s lists
▪ Followed by the market in general
▪ And finally, the rest of the world
o People like to support that which they help create
▪ “I’m almost done creating this product but can you tell me what
your number one question/issue is with XYZ?”
o Ask people for feedback in your market - what do they need to learn to
really change their lives?
o Once they get involved and feel like they’re helping you create the product
they are more likely to support, defend, and get involved in Prelaunch
process
o A little mystery in your pre-pre is good - get people to try and piece together
what’s coming

o In the book The Tipping Point by Malcolm Gladwell:
▪ Mavens are people that love to collect information
▪ Connectors are people that are active in social media
o Persons of influence: Who has real influence and would be good to get
involved?
o People love scarce information and love to tell people about it

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
The Pre-Prelaunch or as we’d like say, call it the pre-pre and as the name
implies, this happens before your Prelaunch even starts.
This is really under the radar before anyone even knows what’s going on.
Generally, people don’t have a hint what you’re really leading into. This is the pre-pre,
this is where you really identify objections and gather testimonials.
Your going to want to use the Pre-Prelaunch to warm your list. You
might even start attracting partners and really, it’s about starting to build
buzz ahead of the launch. This is where you lay the groundwork for your
launch.
We usually start off with what I call “the shot across the bow.” This is an old
naval term and it would be like, if one navy ship is chasing down another ship or maybe
the coastguard’s ship is chasing down a boat, they would often fire a shot in front of the
other boat, land the shot in the water just to warn them that someone is coming so I like
to call this “the shot across the bow.” The essence of the pre-pre is that you’re letting
your market know that something is coming but you’re not actually trying to tell them.
In fact, a big part of message is that the product is not quite ready yet. If your
product isn't even ready, you can’t actually be selling it yet, right? So you remember that
BS Detector that we talked about in the sideways sales letter? The BS Detector, there’s
that red zone, you want to avoid that red zone and when in you’re in the pre-pre, you’ll
see that it’s very easy to avoid that red zone in the pre-pre. Be sure you’re not actually
even selling anything to anyone and when you start off with that BS Detector in the safe
zone, it’s easier to keep it in that safe zone.
When there’s no hint of a pitch and you tell people that something is coming and
you’re really excited about it, it’s like you might come out and say, “Hey, we’ve been
working on this thing for years. We’re so excited. I can’t wait to bring it to you. Can you
just help us out by filling out this survey before we release this?”
That is a lot friendlier than something like, “Hey, we’ve been working on this
thing for years. I'm really excited about it. Please buy it for me, give me your money.” So
it’s a lot softer and gentler to just often go out and ask for feedback in the market and
that’s usually the essence of the pre-pre.
Let’s talk about the characteristics of buzz, because every market is different with
buzz. Some markets are more e-mail-centric, some markets are more forum-centric,
some markets are more social media or even live event-centric.
Every market is different and your market will be different from mine. In the
internet marketing world, where you teach people about building online businesses,
that’s the market that I primarily sell into now. In that market, it’s a very connected
market and everyone is on multiple list. People go to forums; they all do social media. It’s
a very social media-centric market where everyone has got a social media of present and
most of them are publishing via social media. They’re all on Twitter, they’re all on
Facebook. It’s a super connected market so I need to take that into account when I'm
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
doing my Pre-Prelaunch. Your market may vary.
Buzz travels most easily through channels that have trust and authenticity.
They’re not interested in sharing your advertising or if you’re trying to sell them, they’re
not going to go and tell their buddies about your sales. They’re not going to tell their
buddies about your marking.
It comes down to really creating value and being very, very interactive and being
authentic. The more authentic you are and the more you communicate in these authentic
channels that they have trust, the quicker the buzz is going to spread. I like to always hit
multiple modalities in my Pre-Prelaunch.
A lot of people notice something is coming, I love if I can get on stage and speak
on stage somewhere and I can get to do interviews, maybe I drop a hint via e-mail,
maybe I drop a hint via social media. We will leave a trail of bread crumbs for people to
follow, because some people are going to be paying closer attention and most of the
people you want to reach and start having down spread the buzz. I’d like to think of this
in terms of concentric circles here. It’s my business, me and team right in the middle.
I'm the center of the universe, right? You’re the center of your universe.
When I go out there, in terms of influence, the closest people I’ve got are my
Owners, my PLF Owners. One of the rules of my business, when we don’t talk about
clients, we don’t about customers, we talk about our Owners and when we talk about our
Owners, this one of the very few rules I have in my company is, you always have to
capitalize the word “Owners,” when we’re writing about our owners.
In any case, my PLF Owners are the people that I have the closest connection
with. They have been through my training, they love the training, they’ve gotten good
results.
They generally have great affinity to me, I love them. We have this great thing
going with the Owners. So usually, my first hint, my first shot across the bow
is that something is coming will often go out to my Owners. I’ve got my
Owners on the e-mail list. I’ve got my Owners, all in the PLF portal, so I’ll drop a hint
that something is coming in the portal.
Now, beyond that, the next level is often for me, it’s my joint venture
partners, my JVs. This is only going to be if I'm in a JV Launch but I’ll let them know
that something is coming and when I let them know that something is coming, they often
start. They might start Tweeting about it or certain create a blog post about it and they’ll
help create some of the buzz.
The next level beyond that is my social media following. In general, I
consider an e-mail far more powerful than social media, and I have a much bigger e-mail
list. The people that are following me via social media, not all of them, but a lot of them
just don’t actually don’t care, but my most rabid followers of fans are following me via
social media AND will also be on my e-mail list.
Social media is inherently very shareable. I’ll often drop some hints in social
media and in terms of concentric circles, owners are the closest for me, JVs
are the next closest, social media is after that then comes my list, my
prospect list.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
I’ll often drop a hint out to them and then, we get beyond that and we get to the
people on my JVs list. And then, beyond that is the market in general. Everyone in the
market who, maybe if I'm pitching about online marketing, they are not on my JVs list,
they are not on my list, they are not following me, they don’t even know about me but
they’re in the overall market and then, here is the whole rest of the world, probably not
even interested in marketing or business or anything like that.
When I am going into Pre-Prelaunch, I'm mostly going to focus right here on
Owners, I'm going to focus on social media, I’ll drop some hints to JVs.
None of us are really expecting a ton out of that and then, I’ll just hint something
in my list.
I'm not going out to my JVs’ list but what I want, what I'm hoping is that some of
these owners, they’ll start to buzz and they’ll now start to bring some influence out into
the social media area and then some of these social media people, they might start
buzzing and they might start to create some low-lying discussion about what’s going on
with my upcoming launch.
You always have to think of who you influence and the people that are closest to
you. The people in these inner rings are the people that, not only are most interested in
what you have to share but they’re going to be the most interested in actually going out
and sharing it.
Now, one of the things is, people like to support what they help create.
This is a key principle.
People like to support that what they help create. So, if they give you some
feedback, if I go out to my people, to my followers and say, “Hey, I'm almost done with
this product but I want to know, I want to make sure I cover everything in this product.
Can you tell me what your number one question is about growing a list, about learning
how to play rhythm guitar, about how to build your massage tracks?”
Whatever niche you are in, if you go out and ask people for feedback and what
they need to learn to really help change your lives, then by giving you feedback, you’ve
enlisted them in the cost.
They are now part of the creation and people like to support that which they
helped create. If they feel like they’re helping you create a product, they’re going to be
more inclined to tell other people about what you’re doing, to talk about you, to rush to
your defense if anyone is saying bad things about you and then eventually, to get
involved in your whole Prelaunch process and then maybe even buy your product.
That feedback early on is really, really important. In your Pre-Prelaunch, a little
mystery goes a long away. A lot of times, I’ll structure my Pre-Prelaunch so I'm dropping
bread crumbs here and there and people try to piece it together and once they piece it
together, then it’s almost like a scavenger hunt and once they start to put the pieces
together, they love to tell others about it.
They love having the feeling that they have some insider knowledge... but having
insider knowledge is only great if you can actually go and share it with someone. So,
people are going to feel great about connecting the breadcrumbs together and now,
they’ve got a secret and then they want to go out and share it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 8 --

8
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
If you can string these breadcrumbs together and create a little mystery, it can go
a long way towards creating, getting the buzz in your launch, in your Pre-Prelaunch.
I like to think about like in Malcolm Gladwell’s book, The Tipping Point, he
talked about Mavens and Connectors, and Mavens are people that just love to collect
data and information, they’ve already become expert. Connectors are people that they
know lots and lots and lots of other people. They have big followings in social media.
A lot of people that you see publishing very actively in social media, they are
connectors, they really enjoy being connectors. Those Mavens and Connectors are people
that I'm trying to get hooked into my pre-pre and get them excited.
I am looking for persons of influence within my market or niche. These days,
almost everyone is a publisher so who’s got the real influence? Who publishes the blogs
or social media? How can I get them involved in my pre-pre? How can I turn them into
champions and throw at least some breadcrumbs for them, or maybe it’s more overt with
them?
People love to know a secret. People love scarce information and if they have
scarce information, they love to tell other people about it and that all helps to build the
buzz.
Now, I know I'm at the thirty thousand-foot level on this but we’re about to drill
down really deep. I have some examples for you in the next few videos. You’ll see how
I’ve done this, how I used the Pre-Prelaunch in my launches and how effective they’ve
been. You’ll see how you’ve done it a variety of ways and gotten different effects from it.
